summary: Rough audience recording of a historic Brooklyn run, yet the show dazzles with a smoking China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider and a monster The Other One.
review: Despite the dismal, low‑fidelity audience tape, listeners repeatedly praise this four‑night Brooklyn stint for its high energy, tight playing and memorable moments like a scorching China Cat Sunflower > Rider sequence and an epic The Other One. The performance outweighs the technical flaws, making it a must‑hear for Deadheads who value historic vibe over pristine sound.

ai_rating.rationale: Show quality is consistently praised (many 4–5 star reviews) while recording quality is uniformly described as poor; weighting the strong musical performance (70%) against the weak tape quality (30%) yields a rating around 3.8.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Multiple reviewers call the sound "dismal," "rough," "muffled" and note low fidelity, but agree it is listenable enough to capture the performance.

standout_songs: China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider
show_quality.setlist_flow: Four consecutive nights with two electric sets and encores each night; reviewers highlight the high energy, historic vibe and strong flow despite modest audience sizes.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly praised for hanging out with fans, great guitar presence and overall charisma.
